description: Senior with no SAT/ACT scores in their profile evaluated against Ohio State (KB acceptance ~60.6%, ACCESSIBLE). The algorithm's test_strategy.recommendation must NOT be 'Submit' — there's nothing to submit. Phase 2c-2 catches the LLM-default-to-Submit bug surfaced in production 2026-05-04.
business_rationale: A test-optional admissions strategy is one of the highest-stakes calls the algorithm makes — telling a scoreless student to 'Submit' is operationally nonsense (they have nothing). Probe of saved fits on 2026-05-04 showed MIT and UF both recommending Submit despite the synthetic profile carrying no scores. The post-processor override + this archetype's assertion together prevent the regression from re-shipping.
tests: Profile carries no SAT or ACT scores — the algorithm should recognize test-optional context
test_strategy.recommendation must NOT be 'Submit' (Don't Submit or Consider Submitting are valid)
Selectivity ceiling: 60.6% acceptance forces SAFETY (covered by the per-school invariants)
All 8 advisory blocks present and non-empty
